prose:
Method–provenance block
Property: Fukui reactivity indices (f⁺, f⁻, dual descriptor) and most electrophilic/nucleophilic sites for nitrobenzene.
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` → B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density fitting) in PySCF 2.4.0 via chemkit.
Geometry provenance: single-point charges on the user-supplied nitrobenzene.xyz (14 atoms); no further optimisation (DFT//input-XYZ).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent requested).
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet (charge = 0, multiplicity = 1). The N-1 (cation) and N+1 (anion) states were both run as doublets (multiplicity = 2) per default protocol.
Convergence & warnings: SCF cycles for all three charge states converged (integrity.status = ok, trustworthy = true). Warnings returned by the engine (verbatim):
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• common header present
Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (engine subcommand `fukui`) calling PySCF.
Reproducibility: calculation invoked as:
/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 --accept-defaults --stdout json /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/nitrobenzene.xyz
Live run log streamed to the .out file in the current directory during execution (path echoed at start). Key result JSON: /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp8p8l67fr/live.json

Result
The most electrophilic site is C4 (atom index 3) with f⁺ = 0.49641782.
The most nucleophilic site is C5 (atom index 4) with f⁻ ≈ 0.1072882.
The dual descriptor highlights C4 (Δf = +0.51070985) as the most electrophilic and H10 (atom index 9) as the most nucleophilic centre.

Headline number (reported under `value`): f⁺ for the most electrophilic atom (C4) = 0.49641782
